text: 24. TERMINATION FOR CONVENIENCE: Contractor has the right to terminate this subcontract in whole or in part at any time for Contractor's convenience by giving a written notice of termination to Subcontractor. In such event, Subcontractor may claim only  properly  documented  out-of-pocket,  direct  termination  costs  plus  payment  of  any  amount  due  for  the  work  already performed  to  the  date  of  termination  in  accordance  with  the  Subcontract  Documents,  and  any  such  payment  shall  constitute Subcontractor's  sole  and  exclusive  remedy  in  the  event  of  a  termination  for  convenience.  Under  no  circumstances  shall Subcontractor  be  entitled  to  any  unrecovered  overhead  or  loss  of  anticipated  profits  on  work  not  performed,  and  under  no circumstances shall the cumulative amount of any payment due Subcontractor in the event of a termination for convenience and all previous payments to Subcontractor under the subcontract exceed the Subcontract Amount. Upon Contractor's written request, any specially fabricated materials or specially prepared products unique to this subcontract, any partially completed work or work in progress, or raw materials, or any equipment to be incorporated in the Project, shall be held by Subcontractor for disposition in accordance with Contractors written instruction. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Contractor reserves the right to terminate all or any part of the undelivered or uncompleted portion of this subcontract in accordance with paragraph 25 below, without liability, in addition to Contractor's other rights and remedies, if Subcontractor breaches any of the terms and conditions herein.
